mnemonics: The Unchecked ones are the RTE Family: They Run The Engine (and cause most runtime errors). The Checked ones are like IOUs: You Owe them handling (for Input/Output and other external operations).
keyPoints: Checked exceptions are checked at compile-time and must be handled (try-catch) or declared (throws).
Unchecked exceptions (RuntimeException and its subclasses) are not checked at compile-time and typically indicate programming bugs.
Error and its subclasses are serious, unrecoverable problems that are generally not meant to be caught.
commonMistakes: Catching Exception or Throwable instead of more specific exception types, which can mask bugs.
Using exception handling for normal control flow, which is inefficient and makes code harder to read.
Adding overly broad 'throws' declarations instead of handling the exception where it occurs.
